message:
stage 2 phase A: WebGPU backend scaffolding + matmul_int4 WGSL kernel

Lays down the Stage 2 path. `WebGpuBackend` requests a device with
the `shader-f16` feature, uploads every weight tensor from the
shared ONNX graph into persistent GPU storage buffers (same byte
layout the WASM path uses — int4 + f16 scales + packed-uint4 zp +
f16 bias; router scales down-converted f32→f16 at load), and
compiles one compute pipeline so far: `matmul_int4` (f32 x × int4
block W → f32 out).

The shader dequants in-loop and fma-accumulates per-block in f32;
one thread per (t, n) output, 64-thread workgroups. It's the hot
kernel but not the final form — we'll tile for workgroup-shared
X later. Remaining kernels (rms_norm, rope, banded_attention,
swiglu, router_topk, qmoe, embed_lookup_int4, cast_f32→fp16 etc.)
land next; `forward()` still throws.

Parity test: browser-run matmul on real layers.0.attn.q_proj weights
vs the WASM `matmul_f32_x_int4block_out_f32` on identical input —
maxAbs 1.2e-3, rms 5.3e-5, well inside fp16 tolerance. Single
dispatch is slower than WASM (~36 ms vs 0.4 ms) because it eats
first-use pipeline compile + pipe overhead; real gain comes when
the full forward keeps intermediates on the GPU.

Adds `@webgpu/types` dev-dep + tsconfig types entry; exposes
`WebGpuBackend` and `ModelLoader` from the public index so tests
can drive them directly.
